title: CCC: full codebase audit — architecture conformance, legacy cleanup, 'as little as possible' refactor
description: Comprehensive audit of the CCC codebase against the original design philosophy in README.md: 'as much as necessary, as little as possible.' 10-30 reform tasks have been filed recently — this audit should reconcile them against what's actually in the code, identify legacy baggage from earlier generations, and produce a prioritized cleanup plan. Delegate as much as possible to openclaw, tokenhub, linux itself.

principles: As much as necessary, as little as possible
Delegate to: openclaw, tokenhub, linux itself (systemd, cron, ssh, etc.)
Bottom-up from necessity — no over-engineering for cases that don't exist yet
Every component exists because something broke or didn't exist yet
Abstractions extracted from working code, not invented in advance
Heterogeneous hardware must work without privileged network topology assumptions

ccc_api_index_mjs.note: Single file with 8000+ lines. God object. Contains: REST API, auth, secrets, Slack integration, ClawBus, brain orchestration, agent registry, queue management, tunnel management, tokenhub wiring, dashboard HTML/JS inline, email (missing), and more. Should be multiple focused modules.
delegation_candidates: Auth/user management → dedicated auth module (wq-SC-auth-001 started this)
Dashboard HTML/JS → already in dashboard-server, should not be inlined here
Brain/pump logic →.ccc/brain/ already exists, may be duplicated in api/
Slack integration → openclaw handles Slack; CCC shouldn't need its own Slack layer
Tunnel management → SSH + systemd; linux handles this
Tokenhub wiring → tokenhub is the LLM proxy; CCC should just call it, not manage it

config_scatter.note: 50+ process.env vars scattered across index.mjs with no central config schema. wq-CCC-setup-001 proposes ccc.json to consolidate, but that work hasn't started.

recent_reform_tasks.note: wq-CCC-setup-001 (first-run TUI), wq-SC-auth-001 (auth system), wq-API-1775019456431 (config consolidation) + others. Need reconciliation — some may be duplicates or superseded.

questions: What does each route do — is it still needed or superseded?
What can be delegated to openclaw (Slack, messaging, TTS)?
What can be delegated to tokenhub (all LLM calls)?
What can be delegated to linux (systemd, cron, SSH tunnels)?
Which HTML/JS is inlined that should live in dashboard-ui?
Which env vars have no corresponding config UI or docs?
Is brain logic duplicated between brain/index.mjs and api/index.mjs?
Does the pump still match the workqueue schema (SCHEMA.md)?
Is preferred_executor routing still accurate vs current agent capabilities?
Is the Leptos WASM dashboard the canonical UI or is there still HTML inlined in api/?
Does the dashboard-server proxy cover all routes or are some still served directly from the Node API?
Does server.mjs have duplicate auth logic vs the new auth system in f7075c3?
Is ClawChat a dependency of CCC or a standalone service? Should it be decoupled?
Reconcile all wq-*.json files — are any superseded, duplicate, or conflicting?
Are the .bak files safe to delete?
Does the install/onboarding script still match current CCC architecture?
Is the curl|sh install story complete or aspirational?
Which systemd units are current vs legacy?

deliverables: Audit report: what's in the code vs what README says should be there
Deletion candidates: legacy code, dead routes, superseded modules
Delegation map: what should move to openclaw / tokenhub / linux
Consolidated wq list: merge/close duplicate reform tasks, prioritize what's left
Refactor plan: how to break api/index.mjs into focused modules without breaking production
Config schema: proposed ccc.json structure covering all current process.env vars

approach.method: Spawn a sub-agent (Claude Code or Codex) with read-only access to do the audit pass. Output a structured report. Crew reviews before any deletions happen.
approach.constraint: Audit first, delete never without crew review. Production is live on do-host1.
priority_order: 1. Identify what can be deleted entirely (dead code, superseded features)
2. Identify what can be delegated out (shrinks CCC's surface area)
3. Identify what needs to be split into modules (api/index.mjs decomposition)
4. Identify what's missing vs the architecture (config management, setup TUI)
